purpose: Reusable read-only audit context for forensic inspection of the Udemy Enroller project.
Keeps auditors aligned on safety, scope, anti-hallucination rules, and expected deliverables.
applies_to: weave-critic challenging audit completeness and bias
non_goals: No deployment, migration, or database mutation.
No legal advice or platform-appeal drafting.
No design or implementation of automation bypass techniques.

project_summary.business_domain: Browser-automation utility that enrolls users into free Udemy courses, packaged as a web application plus Chrome extension.
primary_users: Learners seeking free Udemy course enrollments
project_summary.current_phase: Phase 1 read-only forensic audit of local codebase and live production site.

tech_stack.name: Python
tech_stack.usage_context: backend automation, web application, CLI entry points

tech_stack.name: Chrome Extension
tech_stack.usage_context: cookie extraction helper for browser automation

tech_stack.name: Tailwind CSS
tech_stack.usage_context: styling for web UI and home page

conventions: Findings must cite exact file paths, URLs, and/or line numbers.
Use severity ratings: critical, high, medium, low, informational.
Never reproduce unredacted secrets or credentials in evidence.
Quotes from code or pages must be verbatim and attributed.

hard: Do not modify any file in the workspace.
Do not deploy, migrate, or mutate databases.
Do not issue POST/mutating requests against the live site.
Do not endorse, design, or improve any technique that bypasses Udemy rate limits, CAPTCHA, credential protections, or Terms of Service.
Do not recommend proxy rotation, stealth automation, account abuse, mass signup, credential harvesting, or unauthorized scraping.
Do not provide legal advice; flag platform-compliance risks for human review.
Redact any secrets, API keys, tokens, or PII discovered before saving evidence.
soft: Prefer curl/httpie/browser DevTools for live inspection.
Respect robots.txt and crawl with delays.
Run tests only in collection or dry-run mode when possible.
Use Lighthouse or PageSpeed Insights for performance metrics.
compatibility: Evidence must be consumable by both humans and the final synthesis task.
JSON and Markdown are the preferred evidence formats.
security_requirements: Secret-detection scan must cover the full repository including history.
Security findings must be flagged critical/high if they expose credentials or PII.
Platform-safety findings must be kept distinct from general security findings.

patterns: Python CLI/web hybrid with main.py and run.py entry points
Browser automation logic in Python modules
GTM container export present at root

known_test_gaps: Coverage data exists in .coverage but has not yet been parsed.
Browser-automation paths may lack deterministic tests.

bottlenecks_known: Browser automation may rely on external page loads (high latency/variability).
SQLite concurrency may limit multi-user scaling.

goals: Run the tool locally or via Docker
Understand code quality and automation safety

business_concepts.definition: Tool that automates enrollment into free Udemy courses.
JSON feed of curated free-course offers.

compliance: Udemy Terms of Service must be respected.
User credential handling must be minimal and secure.

system_assertions.description: No workspace files modified during audit
No unredacted secrets stored in evidence
system_assertions.predicate: manual scan of evidence/ for API keys, passwords, tokens
audit log contains only GET/HEAD requests

system_assertions.description: Final report references all wave-1 evidence files
system_assertions.predicate: every evidence file path appears in Section-1-through-22-Audit-Report.md

why_relevant: Source of truth for public positioning and user instructions
Likely contains automation orchestration and platform-sensitive logic
May include web server bootstrap or automation invocation
Critical for platform-safety and permissions review

gotchas.text: Browser-automation code must be examined for ToS-sensitive patterns; do not assume it is safe.
Chrome extension permissions in manifest.json are a key attack surface and trust signal.
public_deals.json may contain thousands of entries; audit should sample and summarize, not dump.
SQLite database files at project root may contain test or production data; never open unless necessary and never modify.

open_questions.question: Does the live site expose any authenticated/admin endpoints that must not be crawled?
open_questions.context: Read-only audit must avoid touching sensitive paths.

open_questions.question: Are there browser-automation capabilities that interact with Udemy in ways that breach Terms of Service?
open_questions.context: Codebase includes enrollment logic and a chrome-extension directory.

prior_decisions.decision: This is a read-only forensic audit; no remediation code will be produced.
rationale: Prevents accidental mutation of production-adjacent repository.
linked_constraints: Do not modify any file in the workspace.

prior_decisions.decision: Platform-safety and security findings are the highest priority.
rationale: Tool interacts with a third-party platform under ToS.
Credential/automation safety is a trust-critical domain.
linked_constraints: Do not endorse bypassing rate limits/CAPTCHA/TOS.

evidence_map.claim: Repository uses Python for automation and a web layer
Project includes a Chrome extension for cookie extraction

do_not_re_read: Top-level directory listing already captured in this envelope.
Live site URL and project root path are fixed for this plan.
safe_to_assume: Audit is read-only unless user explicitly changes scope.
Platform-safety constraints remain in force for all downstream tasks.
verify_before_use: All findings must be cross-checked against live site or exact code path.
Assumptions about analytics tools must be confirmed by live crawl.
Security findings involving secrets must be validated before escalation.

expected_output_sections.Section_1: Executive Summary — scope, methodology, highest-severity findings, overall risk score.
expected_output_sections.Section_2: Repository & Architecture Inventory — file tree, languages, frameworks, entry points, config files.
expected_output_sections.Section_3: Live Site Crawl Results — URLs, status codes, headers, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, snapshots.
expected_output_sections.Section_4: Code Quality & Test Health — lint results, coverage, dead code, duplication, critical smells.
expected_output_sections.Section_5: Technical SEO Findings — titles, meta descriptions, canonical, hreflang, headings, links, redirects.
expected_output_sections.Section_6: GEO / AEO / AI-Search Readiness — E-E-A-T, citation signals, structured-answer potential, llms.txt.
expected_output_sections.Section_7: Content & Trust Signals — page copy, disclaimers, legal/ToS links, brand voice, conversion clarity.
expected_output_sections.Section_8: Structured Data / Schema Markup — JSON-LD inventory, errors, gaps, recommendations.
expected_output_sections.Section_9: UI/UX Heuristics — layout, navigation, CTAs, responsiveness, visual consistency.
expected_output_sections.Section_10: Accessibility Audit — WCAG 2.1 AA findings, automated + manual checks.
expected_output_sections.Section_11: Performance Audit — Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse, assets, caching, CDN.
expected_output_sections.Section_12: Security & Privacy Findings — secrets scan, headers, injections, PII, dependencies.
expected_output_sections.Section_13: Browser Automation & Platform Safety — ToS-sensitive logic, extension permissions, rate-limit/CAPTCHA bypass.
expected_output_sections.Section_14: Analytics & Tracking Audit — GTM/GA4/Clarity inventory, consent, dataLayer.
expected_output_sections.Section_15: Build, Test & Deployment Stability — Docker, CI/CD, dependency resolution, reproducibility.
expected_output_sections.Section_16: Static Search / Red-Flag Inventory — grep-based anti-patterns, TODOs, unsafe constructs.
expected_output_sections.Section_17: Risk Register — ranked issues with likelihood, impact, evidence paths, owner.
expected_output_sections.Section_18: Compliance & Safety Cross-Cut — Udemy ToS, data handling, user safety.
expected_output_sections.Section_19: Prioritized Remediation Roadmap — quick wins, medium fixes, strategic items.
expected_output_sections.Section_20: Evidence Index — full list of evidence files and how to read them.
expected_output_sections.Section_21: Assumptions & Limitations — what was not tested and why.
expected_output_sections.Section_22: Next Steps — recommended Phase 2 activities (if any).
